mission.title: prometheus: Docker engine swarm api service discovery
mission.description: Docker engine swarm api service discovery. Requested by 39+ users.

steps.title: Check current prometheus deployment
steps.description:
Verify your prometheus version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
helm list -n prometheus 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working prometheus installation.

steps.title: Review prometheus configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus
```
In docker 1.12, the docker engine will ship with swarm mode built in. This means that it is now possible to stand up a swarm cluster using a bunch of nodes with just docker installed.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Docker engine swarm api service discovery
steps.description:
I'm closing the issue as unfortunately, we are currently not accepting new integrations.  [ContainerSolutions/prometheus-swarm-discovery](https://github.com/ContainerSolutions/prometheus-swarm-discovery) is listed in the Prometheus
```yaml
- job_name: 'cadvisor'
    dns_sd_configs:
    - names:
      - 'tasks.cadvisor'
      type: 'A'
      port: 8080

  - job_name: 'node-exporter'
    dns_sd_configs:
    - names:
      - 'tasks.node-exporter'
      type: 'A'
      port: 9100
```

steps.title: Upgrade prometheus to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade prometheus:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade prometheus prometheus/prometheus --namespace prometheus
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus
helm list -n prometheus
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Docker engine swarm api service discovery" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I'm closing the issue as unfortunately, we are currently not accepting new integrations.  [ContainerSolutions/prometheus-swarm-discovery](https://github.com/ContainerSolutions/prometheus-swarm-discovery) is listed in the Prometheus [documentation](https://prometheus.io/docs/operating/integrations/#file-service-discovery) to integrate Docker Swarm with the file service discovery.
codeSnippets:
- job_name: 'cadvisor'
    dns_sd_configs:
    - names:
      - 'tasks.cadvisor'
      type: 'A'
      port: 8080

  - job_name: 'node-exporter'
    dns_sd_configs:
    - names:
      - 'tasks.node-exporter'
      type: 'A'
      port: 9100

docker \
  service create --name node-exporter \
  --mode global \
  --network monitoring \
  --label com.docker.stack.namespace=monitoring \
  --container-label com.docker.stack.namespace=monitoring \
  --mount type=bind,source=/proc,target=/host/proc \
  --mount type=bind,source=/sys,target=/host/sys \
  --mount type=bind,source=/,target=/rootfs \
  --mount type=bind,source=/etc/hostname,target=/etc/host_hostname \
  -e HOST_HOSTNAME=/etc/host_hostname \
  basi/node-exporter:v0.1.1 \
  -collector.procfs /host/proc \
  -collector.sysfs /host/sys \
  -collector.filesystem.ignored-mount-points "^/(sys|proc|dev|host|etc)($|/)" \
  --collector.textfile.directory /etc/node-exporter/ \
  --collectors.enabled="conntrack,diskstats,entropy,filefd,filesystem,loadavg,mdadm,meminfo,netdev,netstat,sta

docker \
  service create \
  --mode global \
  --name docker-exporter \
  --network monitoring \
  --publish 4999 \
  basi/socat:v0.1.0

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
